tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.arguments:
{"device_identifier":"FortiGate7.0.14-1","command":"config firewall address
edit "UbuntuComputer-192.168.1.3"
set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255
next
end
show firewall address UbuntuComputer-192.168.1.3
config firewall policy
show | grep -f log-ubuntucomputer-outbound"}

tool_calls.name: send_command_to_device
tool_calls.content:
config firewall address

FortiGate-VM64-KVM (address) # edit "UbuntuComputer-192.168.1.3"

FortiGate-VM64-KVM (UbuntuComputer-1~1.3) # set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.2 55

FortiGate-VM64-KVM (UbuntuComputer-1~1.3) # next

FortiGate-VM64-KVM (address) # end

FortiGate-VM64-KVM # show firewall address UbuntuComputer-192.168.1.3
config firewall address
    edit "UbuntuComputer-192.168.1.3"
        set uuid 7f58a220-29e4-51f1-4495-f3f058d082f2
        set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255
    next
end

FortiGate-VM64-KVM # config firewall policy

FortiGate-VM64-KVM (policy) # show | grep -f log-ubuntucomputer-outbound
config firewall policy
    edit 2
        set name "log-ubuntucomputer-outbound" <---
        set uuid 837ebb5a-29e4-51f1-b9eb-225adb439c5f
        set srcintf "port2"
        set dstintf "port1"
        set action accept
        set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer-192.168.1.3"
        set dstaddr "all"
        set schedule "always"
        set service "ALL"
        set logtraffic all
        set comments "Log outbound traffic from UbuntuComputer to detect repeated exfiltration attempts"
    next
end

FortiGate-VM64-KVM (policy) # 
FortiGate-VM64-KVM (policy) #
